授業目的:
This intensive 7-week course is
designed for students entering family businesses as future leaders. Using
Problem-Based Learning (PBL), coaching, and reflective practice, you will
develop critical self-awareness and create a Personal Leadership Development
Plan (PLDP) tailored to your family business succession context.

The course integrates
evidence-based leadership frameworks (Leadership Practices Inventory and Gibbs'
Reflective Cycle) with family business succession challenges. You will emerge
with actionable strategies for your leadership development and a concrete plan
aligned with your succession timeline.
到達目標:
(a) Conduct self-assessment
using the Leadership Practices Inventory (LPI) and articulate leadership
strengths and development areas.

(b) Apply Gibbs' Reflective
Cycle to analyze leadership experiences and extract learning for future
application.

(c) Design a comprehensive
Personal Leadership Development Plan with specific goals, strategies, and
timeline aligned with family business succession pathway.

(d) Demonstrate enhanced
self-awareness regarding family business leadership challenges including family
dynamics, credibility-building, and incumbent-successor relationships.
授業方法:
In-person sessions with PBL
approach, individual coaching (3 sessions), and workshop time. All assignments
are individual - no group work. Workload: approximately 3-4 hours per week
outside class7 weeks, 2 sessions/week

第1回:
Session 1: Introduction & Frameworks

Course overview and assessment structure. Introduction to PBL and reflective practice. Overview of LPI and Gibbs' Reflective Cycle. Introduction to family business succession challenges.
Gibbs' Reflective Cycle overview: https://www.ed.ac.uk/reflection/reflectors-toolkit
第2回:
Session 2: Leadership Self-Assessment

Complete Leadership Practices Inventory (LPI) in class. Understanding the five practices of exemplary leadership. Optional 360-degree feedback setup.
第3回:
Session 3: PBL Cycle 1 - Understanding My Leadership

Review LPI results. Problem: 'What are my leadership strengths and blind spots?' Individual research planning using provided readings.
Kouzes & Posner article excerpt on LPI practices
第4回:
Session 4: PBL Cycle 1 - Resolution & Coaching

Brief presentations on leadership profile (5 min each). Peer dialogue. Establishing development priorities. Individual coaching appointments.

Portfolio Entry 1 Due:

'My Leadership Profile' (500 words) Friday 5pm
Develop Portfolio entry following advice in class
第5回:
Session 5: Family Business Context

Understanding dual systems (family & business). Successor-incumbent dynamics. Managing family relationships while leading professionally. Case examples.
第6回:
Session 6: PBL Cycle 2 - My Family Business

Problem: 'What are the unique leadership requirements in MY family business?' Share insights from family conversations. Build development strategy framework.
Brief conversation with family business leader about their expectations (notes only)
第7回:
Session 7: Development Planning Framework

Introduction to 70-20-10 model (70% experience, 20% relationships, 10% training). SMART goals workshop. Building your development activities.
Introduction to 70-20-10 model (70% experience, 20% relationships, 10% training). SMART goals workshop. Building your development activities.
第8回:
Session 8: PBL Resolution & Coaching

Present analysis of family context and development needs. Second individual coaching checkpoint. Begin PLDP drafting.

Portfolio Entry 2 Due

 'My Family Business Context' (500 words)  Friday 5pm
第9回:
Session 9: Succession as a Process

Understanding succession timeline and phases. Managing emotional and psychological dimensions. Creating your succession roadmap.
第10回:
Session 10: PLDP Workshop Session

Dedicated work time on Personal Leadership Development Plans. Individual consultations available. Peer feedback optional. Third coaching checkpoint.

Preparation:

•        Bring PLDP draft in progress for consultation
第11回:
Session 11: PLDP Development (continued)

Continued work on PLDPs. Focus on accountability mechanisms and realistic timelines. Review of assessment criteria.
第12回:
Session 12: PLDP Practice Presentations (NON-GRADED)

Practice presentations of PLDP (10 minutes each). Formative feedback from instructor and peers. Opportunity to refine before graded presentations in Sessions 13 & 14. This session provides a low-stakes environment to test your presentation and receive constructive feedback.

Bring to Session 12:

•        PLDP draft (does not need to be perfect - this is for feedback)

•        Presentation slides or outline

•        Specific questions for feedback
第13回:
Session 13: PLDP Final Presentations - Part 1 (GRADED)

Final PLDP presentations (10 minutes each). Graded presentations with Q&A. Submit final written PLDP before presenting.

Assignment Due (BEFORE presentation):

•        Final PLDP (maximum 2000 words) - revised based on Session 12 feedback

•        Presentation slides
第14回:
Session 14: PLDP Final Presentations - Part 2 & Course Conclusion (GRADED)

Remaining graded PLDP presentations. Course synthesis and reflection on learning journey. Next steps for implementing your PLDP.

Portfolio Entry 3 Due (Friday 5pm following Session 14):

•        'My Leadership Journey' - Final reflection (500 words)

教科書/Required texts0:
No required textbook.

All readings will be provided as excerpts or links. Total reading: approximately 3-4 hours across 7 weeks.
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: Rubric will be provided in advance
成績評価Grading2: プレゼンテーション・発表／Presentation (07)
